LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT PG&E CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - PG&E'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 3, DENIED AS TO ISSUE 4, AND TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT AS TO ISSUE 2. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN AS TO THE WRONGFUL TERMINATION CLAIM. DEFENDANT RELIES ON THE DECLARATION OF PAUL ROLLER TO ARGUE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS TERMINATED BECAUSE OF THE POWER TOOLS INCIDENT AND SUBSEQUENT COVER UP. HOWEVER, MR. ROLLER DOES NOT STATE THE PRECISE REASON FOR THE TERMINATION. THE UNDISPUTED FACTS SHOW THAT THE DEFAMATION CLAIM IS TIME-BARRED PER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 340(C) AND BARRED BY THE COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE OF CIVIL CODE 47(C). PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE OF THE HEARING IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077 =(302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-26-2013 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-26-2012 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-26-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL WELCH, TEL. NO. 399-1500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CATHERINE S. NASSER OF JONES DAY LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 626-3939 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT PG & E.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PG&E CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - PG&E's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 3, denied as to issue 4, and taken off calendar as moot as to issue 2. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden as to the wrongful termination claim. Defendant relies on the declaration of Paul Roller to argue that plaintiff was terminated because of the power tools incident and subsequent cover up. However, Mr. Roller does not state the precise reason for the termination. The undisputed facts show that the defamation claim is time-barred per Code of Civil Procedure 340(c) and barred by the common interest privilege of Civil Code 47(c). Plaintiff's request for a continuance of the hearing is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(302/HEK)
